methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 15 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Defense & Security. Range: $500M - $9.2B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Anduril Industries
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (SPAC), post-money $78B+

data.narrative:
True Anomaly, a Defense & Security company at the SPAC stage with a current valuation of $2.2B, was evaluated against a peer universe of 15 same-sector comparable companies spanning both public and private stages. The comp set includes 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms, with top private comparables including Anduril Industries, Saronic ($9.2B, Series D), Flock Safety ($7.5B, Series E), Shield AI ($5.6B, Series G), and Chaos Industries ($4.5B, Series D).

The peer-median method was applied as the primary triangulation approach. The median valuation across the 15 defense & security peers was $1.5B, with a range spanning $500M to $9.2B. This median directly reflects the distribution of like-stage and similarly-positioned companies in the sector.

The triangulated analysis implies a valuation range of $1.3B to $1.7B, with a median of $1.5B. True Anomaly's current $2.2B valuation sits 47% above the peer median, warranting scrutiny of whether the SPAC structure, founder reputation (Even Rogers), or differentiated technology justify a premium to comparables. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory relative to peers, defensibility of technology, and customer concentration risk within defense procurement. The single-method approach limits conviction; additional revenue or margin-based analysis would strengthen the range.
